title: Cerastium danguyi
taxonomy: Cerastium danguyi belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and morphological features. As a member of the Caryophyllaceae family, Cerastium danguyi is closely related to other flowering plants such as carnations and forget-me-nots. The genus Cerastium, to which Cerastium danguyi belongs, is a group of annual and perennial plants commonly known as chickweeds. Cerastium danguyi is a rare and endemic species found in certain regions.

intro: Cerastium danguyi, also known as Danguy's chickweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cerastium danguyi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Cerastium danguyi include infusions, decoctions, and creams, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues.
